narrative: As a Black male applicant with a solid undergraduate GPA of 3.50—above the typical 3.21 average—your law school application stood out in some ways, but the admissions model focused on predicting your chances of passing the bar exam on the first try. The model assessed your overall profile and estimated a 67% probability that you would fail the bar exam, which crossed the threshold for rejection since the school prioritizes applicants likely to succeed and avoid high failure risks. This prediction hinged on just a few key factors from your background, with your Year 3 school decile ranking as the top influence, pushing strongly toward a failure prediction because your position in the 2nd decile was notably below the average 5.53. Next in importance was your LSAT score of 26, well under the dataset average of 36.70, which also significantly nudged the model toward expecting bar failure. On a more positive note, your undergraduate GPA provided the strongest counterbalance, pulling the prediction toward success since it exceeded what most students achieve. Compared to typical students, your middle family income bracket aligned with the norm, and your full-time student status matched the overwhelming majority at 92.7%, so those didn't sway the decision much. While your early Year 1 decile of 1st place showed promise, it wasn't enough to offset the later academic signals the model weighed most heavily. This isn't a judgment on your potential as a Black man pursuing law—many with similar backgrounds overcome these hurdles—but the model saw your profile as riskier than most admitted applicants who tend to have stronger mid-to-late academic deciles and higher LSATs. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score closer to or above the average, as that could dramatically shift future predictions. Strengthening your recent academic performance through additional coursework or a post-bac program might also help elevate your decile rankings. Reach out to admissions advisors for targeted feedback, and explore schools with holistic reviews that value your unique story alongside these metrics. With focused improvements, you're positioned to reapply stronger next cycle.
